critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — ACA COMMUNITY RATING AND AGE/REGION: If this model informs premium pricing in any way, using age and region alongside health status features could blur the line between permissible and impermissible rating factors.

C2 — PRIOR-YEAR CLAIMS AS HEALTH STATUS: Prior-year claims is a direct health status proxy. Using it in any model that touches premium pricing could violate ACA community rating rules.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — The model does not set member-facing premiums. It informs internal reserve setting and reinsurance assessment. ACA community rating rules apply to external premium determination. The actuarial memo, reviewed by outside counsel, confirms this distinction. Using age and region in an internal cost prediction model is not a community rating violation.

C2 — Prior-year claims is an impermissible individual rating factor for member-facing premiums. It is not impermissible for internal actuarial cost prediction. The model never touches individual premium determination. Outside counsel confirmed this analysis.
adjudication_raw: Both points resolve to defense after round 1. The key legal distinction — ACA community rating rules apply to member-facing premiums, not to internal actuarial models — is confirmed by outside counsel review. The committee's concerns are based on a misidentification of the applicable regulatory framework.
